name: Japan
funFact: Japan is made up of over 6,800 islands, though only about 430 are inhabited.
history: Japan has never been colonized; its imperial line is traditionally dated to 660 BCE, making it one of the world's oldest continuous monarchies.

name: China
funFact: The Great Wall of China is not a single wall but a network of walls stretching over 21,000 km (13,050 mi).
history: The People's Republic of China was founded in 1949 after the Chinese Civil War, but Chinese statehood traces back over 3,000 years.

name: India
funFact: India has the world's largest postal network, with over 150,000 post offices.
history: India gained independence from the United Kingdom on August 15, 1947, ending nearly two centuries of British rule.

name: Indonesia
funFact: Indonesia is the largest archipelago on Earth, with more than 17,000 islands.
history: Indonesia declared independence from the Netherlands in 1945, though the Dutch didn't formally recognize it until 1949.

name: South Korea
funFact: South Korea has one of the fastest average internet speeds in the world.
history: Korea was liberated from 35 years of Japanese colonial rule in 1945, and the Republic of Korea was established in the south in 1948.

name: Thailand
funFact: Bangkok's full ceremonial name is the longest place name in the world, with 168 letters.
history: Thailand is the only Southeast Asian country never colonized by a European power, having skillfully played Britain and France off each other.

name: Vietnam
funFact: Vietnam is the world's second-largest exporter of coffee, after Brazil.
history: Vietnam declared independence from France in 1945, but full sovereignty over a unified country wasn't achieved until 1975 after decades of war.

name: Philippines
funFact: The Philippines is made up of 7,641 islands and is one of the most biodiverse nations on Earth.
history: The Philippines gained independence from the United States in 1946, after nearly 400 years of Spanish rule and almost 50 years of American administration.

name: Saudi Arabia
funFact: Saudi Arabia has no rivers — it is the largest country in the world without one.
history: The modern Kingdom of Saudi Arabia was founded in 1932 by Ibn Saud, who unified the territory after decades of conquest.

name: Turkey
funFact: Istanbul is the only major city in the world that sits on two continents, Europe and Asia.
history: The Republic of Turkey was founded in 1923 by Mustafa Kemal Atatürk from the remnants of the Ottoman Empire.

name: Israel
funFact: The Dead Sea, on Israel's border, is the lowest point on Earth's land surface.
history: Israel declared independence in 1948, immediately following the expiration of the British Mandate for Palestine.
note: Jerusalem's status as capital is internationally disputed; most countries keep their embassies in Tel Aviv.

name: Palestine
funFact: Palestine has no currency of its own — it mainly uses the Israeli shekel, with the Jordanian dinar also legal tender in the West Bank.
history: Palestine declared statehood in 1988, though it remains only partially recognized and much of its territory has been under Israeli administration since 1967.
note: The State of Palestine designates East Jerusalem as its capital, but does not administer it; Ramallah is the seat of government in practice. Palestine is a UN observer state, recognized by most UN member states but not universally.

name: Iran
funFact: Iran is home to one of the world's oldest continuous civilizations, dating back over 5,000 years.
history: Iran was never formally colonized; Persian statehood stretches back over 2,500 years, and the modern Islamic Republic was founded in 1979.

name: Pakistan
funFact: Pakistan has the world's highest paved international road, the Karakoram Highway.
history: Pakistan was created in 1947 when British India was partitioned, gaining independence from the United Kingdom the same day as India.

name: Bangladesh
funFact: Bangladesh hosts the largest mangrove forest in the world, the Sundarbans.
history: Bangladesh gained independence from Pakistan in 1971 after a nine-month war of liberation.

name: United Kingdom
funFact: The UK has no single written constitution — its laws come from statutes, court rulings and conventions.
history: The United Kingdom was formed by the Acts of Union — England and Scotland unified in 1707, joined by Ireland in 1801 (most of which later became independent).

name: France
funFact: France is the most visited country in the world, welcoming around 90 million tourists a year.
history: France's modern republic dates to 1792, though the current Fifth Republic was established in 1958 under Charles de Gaulle.

name: Germany
funFact: Germany has over 1,500 types of beer and more than 20,000 castles.
history: Germany was first unified as a nation-state in 1871; the modern Federal Republic was founded in 1949 and reunified with East Germany in 1990.

name: Italy
funFact: Italy has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country.
history: Italy was unified into a single kingdom in 1861 after centuries of fragmented city-states and foreign rule.

name: Spain
funFact: Spain produces around 44% of the world's olive oil — more than any other country.
history: Spain was unified through the 1469 marriage of Ferdinand and Isabella, with the Reconquista completed in 1492.

name: Portugal
funFact: Lisbon is older than Rome — it was a trading port centuries before the Roman Empire.
history: Portugal is one of Europe's oldest nation-states, with borders essentially unchanged since 1143.

name: Netherlands
funFact: About a third of the Netherlands lies below sea level, protected by dikes and pumps.
history: The Netherlands declared independence from Spain in 1581, formally recognized in 1648 under the Peace of Westphalia after the Eighty Years' War.

name: Greece
funFact: Greece has around 6,000 islands and islets, of which only about 227 are inhabited.
history: Greece gained independence from the Ottoman Empire in 1830 after a war of independence that began in 1821.

name: Russia
funFact: Russia is the largest country on Earth, spanning 11 time zones.
history: The Russian Federation was established in 1991 following the collapse of the Soviet Union, though the Russian state traces back to Kievan Rus in the 9th century.

name: Poland
funFact: Poland is home to Europe's last remaining lowland primeval forest, Bialowieza.
history: Poland regained independence in 1918 after being partitioned and erased from the map by Russia, Prussia and Austria for over a century.

name: Sweden
funFact: Sweden's right of public access lets anyone roam, camp and forage on most land.
history: Sweden became fully independent from the Kalmar Union with Denmark in 1523 under King Gustav Vasa.

name: Norway
funFact: In parts of northern Norway the sun does not set for weeks during the midnight-sun season.
history: Norway peacefully dissolved its union with Sweden in 1905, gaining full independence.

name: Switzerland
funFact: Switzerland has four official languages: German, French, Italian and Romansh.
history: Switzerland traces its founding to a 1291 alliance of three cantons, with independence from the Holy Roman Empire formally recognized in 1648.

name: Ireland
funFact: Ireland is known as the Emerald Isle for its lush green landscape, fed by frequent rain.
history: Ireland gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1922 after the Anglo-Irish War, becoming a republic fully in 1949.

name: United States
funFact: The U.S. has no official national language at the federal level.
history: The United States declared independence from Great Britain in 1776, with sovereignty confirmed by the 1783 Treaty of Paris.

name: Canada
funFact: Canada has the longest coastline of any country, at over 202,000 km (125,500 mi).
history: Canada became a self-governing dominion in 1867 and only gained full constitutional independence from the United Kingdom in 1982.

name: Mexico
funFact: Mexico City is sinking up to 50 cm a year because it was built on a drained lake bed.
history: Mexico gained independence from Spain in 1821 after an eleven-year war that began with Miguel Hidalgo's 1810 Cry of Dolores.

name: Cuba
funFact: Classic 1950s American cars are still common on Cuban streets after decades of trade embargo.
history: Cuba gained independence from Spain in 1898, though the U.S. controlled the island until it became a formal republic in 1902.

name: Guatemala
funFact: Guatemala's currency, the quetzal, is named after its national bird.
history: Guatemala gained independence from Spain in 1821 as part of the Federal Republic of Central America, becoming fully separate in 1841.

name: Costa Rica
funFact: Costa Rica abolished its army in 1948 and invests the savings in education and health.
history: Costa Rica gained independence from Spain in 1821, along with the rest of Central America.

name: Panama
funFact: The Panama Canal lets ships pass between the Atlantic and Pacific, saving an 8,000-mile trip.
history: Panama gained independence from Colombia in 1903, with U.S. support tied to construction of the Panama Canal.

name: Jamaica
funFact: Jamaica is the birthplace of reggae music and the genre's global ambassador, Bob Marley.
history: Jamaica gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1962, ending over 300 years of British colonial rule.

name: Brazil
funFact: The Amazon rainforest, mostly in Brazil, is the most biodiverse place on the planet.
history: Brazil declared independence from Portugal in 1822, uniquely becoming an empire rather than a republic at first.

name: Argentina
funFact: Argentina is home to both the highest and lowest points in the Southern Hemisphere.
history: Argentina declared independence from Spain in 1816, led in part by liberator José de San Martín.

name: Colombia
funFact: Colombia has more bird species than any other country on Earth.
history: Colombia gained independence from Spain in 1819 following Simón Bolívar's campaign, initially as part of Gran Colombia.

name: Peru
funFact: Machu Picchu, the Inca citadel, sits about 2,430 m (7,970 ft) above sea level in the Andes.
history: Peru declared independence from Spain in 1821, with Spanish forces finally defeated at the Battle of Ayacucho in 1824.

name: Chile
funFact: Chile's Atacama Desert is the driest non-polar desert on Earth — some areas have never recorded rain.
history: Chile declared independence from Spain in 1818 after a war led by Bernardo O'Higgins and José de San Martín.

name: Venezuela
funFact: Venezuela is home to Angel Falls, the world's tallest uninterrupted waterfall at 979 m (3,212 ft).
history: Venezuela was among the first Spanish colonies to declare independence, in 1811, though it wasn't secured until Bolívar's victory in 1821.

name: Ecuador
funFact: Ecuador is named after the Equator, which passes through the country.
history: Ecuador gained independence from Spain in 1822 as part of Gran Colombia, becoming a fully separate republic in 1830.

name: Bolivia
funFact: Bolivia's Salar de Uyuni is the world's largest salt flat, turning into a giant mirror when wet.
history: Bolivia gained independence from Spain in 1825 and is named after liberator Simón Bolívar.

name: Uruguay
funFact: Uruguay was one of the first countries to generate nearly all its electricity from renewables.
history: Uruguay declared independence from Brazil in 1825, with its sovereignty guaranteed by an 1828 treaty between Argentina and Brazil.

name: Egypt
funFact: The Great Pyramid of Giza was the tallest human-made structure for nearly 4,000 years.
history: Egypt gained nominal independence from the United Kingdom in 1922, though British influence persisted until the 1952 revolution.

name: Nigeria
funFact: Nigeria is Africa's most populous country and home to Nollywood, a top-three film industry by output.
history: Nigeria gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1960, uniting dozens of distinct ethnic nations under one flag.

name: South Africa
funFact: South Africa has three capital cities: Pretoria, Cape Town and Bloemfontein.
history: South Africa became a self-governing dominion of the United Kingdom in 1910 and a fully independent republic in 1961.

name: Kenya
funFact: Kenya's Maasai Mara hosts the annual wildebeest migration, one of nature's greatest spectacles.
history: Kenya gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1963 after years of the Mau Mau uprising.

name: Ethiopia
funFact: Ethiopia follows its own calendar with 13 months and is roughly 7–8 years behind the Gregorian one.
history: Ethiopia is Africa's oldest independent country, having never been colonized except for a brief Italian occupation from 1936 to 1941.

name: Morocco
funFact: The medina of Fez is one of the world's largest car-free urban areas.
history: Morocco gained independence from France in 1956, with Spain relinquishing its remaining protectorate zones soon after.

name: Ghana
funFact: Ghana was the first sub-Saharan African nation to gain independence from colonial rule, in 1957.
history: Ghana became the first sub-Saharan African colony to gain independence, from the United Kingdom, in 1957.

name: Tanzania
funFact: Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania is the highest free-standing mountain in the world.
history: Tanzania formed in 1964 when Tanganyika (independent from the UK in 1961) united with Zanzibar (independent in 1963).

name: Algeria
funFact: Algeria is the largest country in Africa, with the Sahara covering over 80% of its land.
history: Algeria gained independence from France in 1962 after an eight-year war that killed hundreds of thousands.

name: Senegal
funFact: Dakar gave its name to the famous Dakar Rally, which originally finished in the city.
history: Senegal gained independence from France in 1960, briefly as part of the short-lived Mali Federation.

name: Australia
funFact: Australia is home to the Great Barrier Reef, the largest living structure visible from space.
history: Australia federated as a commonwealth in 1901 and gained full legislative independence from the United Kingdom with the Statute of Westminster and the 1986 Australia Act.

name: New Zealand
funFact: New Zealand has more sheep than people, by a ratio of roughly five to one.
history: New Zealand became a dominion in 1907 and adopted full independence from the United Kingdom in 1947.

name: Fiji
funFact: Fiji is an archipelago of more than 330 islands, of which about a third are inhabited.
history: Fiji gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1970.

name: Papua New Guinea
funFact: Papua New Guinea has over 800 living languages — the most linguistically diverse country on Earth.
history: Papua New Guinea gained independence from Australia in 1975.

name: Iceland
funFact: Iceland runs almost entirely on renewable geothermal and hydroelectric power.
history: Iceland became a fully independent republic in 1944, dissolving its union with Denmark during World War II.

name: Finland
funFact: Finland has about 188,000 lakes and is nicknamed 'the land of a thousand lakes'.
history: Finland declared independence from Russia in 1917, amid the chaos of the Russian Revolution.

name: Austria
funFact: Vienna has been ranked the world's most liveable city multiple times.
history: Austria became a republic in 1918 after the collapse of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, and regained full sovereignty in 1955 when Allied occupation ended.

name: Belgium
funFact: Belgium produces over 220,000 tonnes of chocolate a year and has more than 1,100 chocolatiers.
history: Belgium gained independence from the Netherlands in 1830 following the Belgian Revolution.

name: United Arab Emirates
funFact: Dubai's Burj Khalifa is the tallest building in the world at 828 m (2,717 ft).
history: The United Arab Emirates was formed in 1971 when seven emirates united after the United Kingdom withdrew from the Gulf.

name: Singapore
funFact: Singapore is one of only three surviving city-states in the world.
history: Singapore gained independence from the United Kingdom via merger with Malaysia in 1963, then became a fully separate republic in 1965 after being expelled from the federation.

name: Malaysia
funFact: The Petronas Towers in Kuala Lumpur were the world's tallest buildings from 1998 to 2004.
history: The Federation of Malaya gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1957; Malaysia was formed in 1963 by merging with Singapore, Sabah and Sarawak.

name: Nepal
funFact: Nepal contains eight of the world's ten highest peaks, including Mount Everest.
history: Nepal was never colonized, having successfully resisted British expansion, though it ceded some territory after the 1814-16 Anglo-Nepalese War.

name: Mongolia
funFact: Mongolia is the most sparsely populated sovereign nation on Earth.
history: Mongolia declared independence from Qing China in 1911 and secured it with Soviet backing in 1921.

name: Kazakhstan
funFact: Kazakhstan is the world's largest landlocked country.
history: Kazakhstan gained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991, the last Soviet republic to declare independence.

name: Ukraine
funFact: Ukraine is the largest country located entirely within Europe.
history: Ukraine gained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991 following a referendum with over 90% support.

name: Czechia
funFact: Czechia has the highest beer consumption per person of any country in the world.
history: Czechoslovakia gained independence from Austria-Hungary in 1918; it peacefully split into the Czech Republic and Slovakia in 1993.

name: Hungary
funFact: Budapest sits on over 100 thermal springs and is known as the 'City of Spas'.
history: Hungary became independent in 1918 as the Austro-Hungarian Empire dissolved at the end of World War I.

name: Denmark
funFact: Denmark is often ranked among the happiest countries in the world.
history: Denmark is home to one of the world's oldest continuous monarchies, with a kingdom traceable to the 10th century.

name: Qatar
funFact: Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup, the first ever held in the Middle East.
history: Qatar gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1971.

name: Cambodia
funFact: Angkor Wat in Cambodia is the largest religious monument in the world.
history: Cambodia gained independence from France in 1953.

name: Sri Lanka
funFact: Sri Lanka was the first country in the world to elect a female head of government, in 1960.
history: Sri Lanka (then Ceylon) gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1948.

name: Tunisia
funFact: Tunisia is home to the ancient city of Carthage, a former great power that rivaled Rome.
history: Tunisia gained independence from France in 1956.

name: Libya
funFact: Libya has the largest proven oil reserves in Africa.
history: Libya gained independence in 1951, the first country to achieve independence through the United Nations, after Italian colonization ended in World War II.

name: Sudan
funFact: Sudan is home to the Nubian pyramids, with more pyramids than Egypt.
history: Sudan gained independence in 1956 from joint British-Egyptian rule.

name: Ivory Coast
funFact: Ivory Coast is the world's largest producer of cocoa, supplying about 40% of global cocoa.
history: Ivory Coast (Côte d'Ivoire) gained independence from France in 1960.

name: Mali
funFact: Mali was home to the Mali Empire, which controlled important trans-Saharan trade routes and was renowned for its wealth in gold.
history: Mali gained independence from France in 1960, briefly federated with Senegal beforehand.

name: Niger
funFact: Niger is home to the W National Park, one of the largest protected wildlife areas in West Africa, shared with Benin and Burkina Faso.
history: Niger gained independence from France in 1960.

name: Guinea
funFact: Guinea has the world's largest reserves of bauxite, the primary ore from which aluminum is extracted.
history: Guinea gained independence from France in 1958, the first French colony in Africa to do so after voting against a new French constitution.

name: Sierra Leone
funFact: Sierra Leone was founded in 1787 as a home for freed slaves, with its capital Freetown named after their freedom.
history: Sierra Leone gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1961.

name: Benin
funFact: Benin is considered the birthplace of Vodou (often called Voodoo), a spiritual practice that influences the culture and religion of the region.
history: Benin (then Dahomey) gained independence from France in 1960.

name: Togo
funFact: Togo is one of the smallest countries in Africa, measuring only about 579 kilometers (360 mi) from north to south.
history: Togo gained independence from France in 1960, having earlier been a German then French/British-administered territory.

name: Burkina Faso
funFact: Burkina Faso means 'land of honest people' in the local Mossi and Dioula languages.
history: Burkina Faso (then Upper Volta) gained independence from France in 1960.

name: Gambia
funFact: Gambia is the smallest independent country in mainland Africa, nearly surrounded by Senegal.
history: The Gambia gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1965.

name: Uganda
funFact: Uganda is home to the source of the White Nile River and has the second-highest concentration of mountain gorillas in the world.
history: Uganda gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1962.

name: Rwanda
funFact: Rwanda is known as the 'Land of a Thousand Hills' due to its rolling terrain and lush green landscape.
history: Rwanda gained independence from Belgium in 1962.

name: Somalia
funFact: Somalia has the longest coastline of any African country, stretching along the Indian Ocean and the Gulf of Aden.
history: Somalia formed in 1960 when British Somaliland and Italian Somaliland each gained independence and united.

name: Eritrea
funFact: Eritrea is one of Africa's youngest nations, having gained independence from Ethiopia in 1993 after a 30-year war.
history: Eritrea gained independence from Ethiopia in 1993 after a 30-year war and a UN-supervised referendum.

name: Djibouti
funFact: Djibouti is located at the gateway to the Red Sea and houses a major strategic port serving the wider region.
history: Djibouti gained independence from France in 1977.

name: Cameroon
funFact: Cameroon is known as 'Africa in miniature' due to its geographical and cultural diversity, from rainforests to deserts.
history: Cameroon gained independence from France in 1960, with the southern part of British Cameroons joining a year later.

name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
funFact: The Democratic Republic of the Congo has the world's second-largest rainforest after the Amazon, covering over 2 million square kilometers (772,000 sq mi).
history: The Democratic Republic of the Congo gained independence from Belgium in 1960, following brutal colonial exploitation dating back to King Leopold II's personal rule.

name: Gabon
funFact: Gabon is one of Africa's most sparsely populated countries, with about 80% of its land covered in rainforest.
history: Gabon gained independence from France in 1960.

name: Chad
funFact: Chad is home to Lake Chad, one of the world's largest freshwater lakes, though it has shrunk significantly due to climate change.
history: Chad gained independence from France in 1960.

name: Central African Republic
funFact: The Central African Republic sits at the geographic center of Africa and is home to vast diamond and gold reserves.
history: The Central African Republic gained independence from France in 1960.

name: Angola
funFact: Angola is the second-largest oil producer in Africa and has significant diamond reserves.
history: Angola gained independence from Portugal in 1975 after a 14-year independence war, followed immediately by decades of civil war.

name: Zimbabwe
funFact: Zimbabwe is home to Victoria Falls, one of the Seven Natural Wonders of the World, shared with Zambia.
history: Zimbabwe (then Rhodesia) gained internationally recognized independence from the United Kingdom in 1980, following a guerrilla war against the white-minority government.

name: Botswana
funFact: Botswana is home to the Kalahari Desert and the Okavango Delta, one of the world's largest inland deltas.
history: Botswana gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1966.

name: Namibia
funFact: Namibia is home to the Namib Desert, one of the oldest deserts in the world, and has some of the highest sand dunes on Earth.
history: Namibia gained independence from South Africa in 1990, one of the last African countries to do so.

name: Mozambique
funFact: Mozambique has a long coastline along the Indian Ocean and is becoming an increasingly popular tourist destination.
history: Mozambique gained independence from Portugal in 1975 after a decade-long liberation war.

name: Zambia
funFact: Zambia is home to Victoria Falls, known locally as Mosi-oa-Tunya, meaning 'The Smoke That Thunders'.
history: Zambia (then Northern Rhodesia) gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1964.

name: Malawi
funFact: Malawi is home to Lake Malawi, one of the largest and most biodiverse freshwater lakes in Africa.
history: Malawi (then Nyasaland) gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1964.

name: Madagascar
funFact: Madagascar is the world's fourth-largest island and has unique wildlife found nowhere else on Earth, including lemurs.
history: Madagascar gained independence from France in 1960.

name: Vanuatu
funFact: Vanuatu is located on the Pacific Ring of Fire and experiences frequent volcanic activity with several active volcanoes.
history: Vanuatu gained independence in 1980 from an unusual joint British-French condominium government.

name: Solomon Islands
funFact: The Solomon Islands archipelago consists of nearly 1,000 islands stretching across 1,400 kilometers (870 mi) of the Pacific Ocean.
history: The Solomon Islands gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1978.

name: Samoa
funFact: Samoa switched which side of the International Date Line it's on in 2011, skipping December 30 entirely to align its trade week with Australia and New Zealand.
history: Samoa gained independence from New Zealand in 1962, the first Pacific island nation to do so in the 20th century.

name: Tonga
funFact: Tonga is one of the few Pacific island nations never colonized by a European power and maintains its own monarchy.
history: Tonga is the only Pacific nation never colonized, ending its status as a British-protected state in 1970.

name: Tuvalu
funFact: Tuvalu is one of the most vulnerable nations to climate change due to rising sea levels, with the highest point only 5 meters (16 ft) above sea level.
history: Tuvalu gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1978.

name: Kiribati
funFact: Kiribati straddles the International Date Line and the equator, making it one of the most geographically dispersed nations.
history: Kiribati gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1979.

name: Federated States of Micronesia
funFact: The Federated States of Micronesia consists of four states spread across the western Pacific Ocean with a total area smaller than Rhode Island.
history: The Federated States of Micronesia gained independence from the United States in 1986, after decades as a UN Trust Territory.

name: Palau
funFact: Palau is famous for Jellyfish Lake, which is home to millions of stingless golden jellyfish found nowhere else on Earth.
history: Palau gained independence from the United States in 1994, the last UN Trust Territory to do so.

name: Marshall Islands
funFact: The Marshall Islands is composed of 29 atolls and 5 islands spread across the Pacific Ocean, with Majuro being one of the largest atolls in the world.
history: The Marshall Islands gained independence from the United States in 1986.

name: Nauru
funFact: Nauru is the smallest republic in the world by both area and population, measuring only 21 square kilometers (8 sq mi).
history: Nauru gained independence in 1968, and remains the world's smallest island nation.

name: Afghanistan
funFact: Afghanistan is home to the Hindu Kush mountains and sits at the crossroads of Central and South Asia, a crucial historical trade route.
history: Afghanistan regained full control of its foreign affairs from the United Kingdom in 1919 after the third Anglo-Afghan War, having never been formally colonized.

name: Myanmar
funFact: Myanmar is home to the Irrawaddy River, one of Southeast Asia's most important waterways, and thousands of golden temples.
history: Myanmar (then Burma) gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1948.

name: Laos
funFact: Laos is the only landlocked country in Southeast Asia and is known for its ancient temples and the Mekong River.
history: Laos gained independence from France in 1953.

name: North Korea
funFact: North Korea is one of the most isolated nations on Earth, with limited internet access and strict government control over information.
history: North Korea was established in 1948, three years after Korea was liberated from 35 years of Japanese colonial rule.

name: Yemen
funFact: Yemen is home to Socotra Island, often called the 'Galápagos of the Indian Ocean' due to its unique and endemic flora.
history: Yemen unified in 1990 from two states: North Yemen, independent from the Ottoman Empire since 1918, and South Yemen, independent from the United Kingdom since 1967.

name: Jordan
funFact: Jordan is home to Petra, a city carved directly into rose-colored sandstone cliffs by the Nabataeans over 2,000 years ago.
history: Jordan gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1946.

name: Lebanon
funFact: Lebanon is home to the Cedars of God, ancient cedar trees that appear on the country's flag and have been revered for thousands of years.
history: Lebanon gained independence from France in 1943.

name: Iraq
funFact: Iraq is home to the ruins of Babylon, one of the most important cities in ancient Mesopotamia and a center of learning and culture.
history: Iraq gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1932.

name: Oman
funFact: Oman is known for its dramatic mountainous terrain and stunning beaches along the Arabian Sea, and was a major maritime trading power.
history: Oman has been continuously independent for centuries, never formally colonized, making it one of the oldest states in the Arab world.

name: Kuwait
funFact: Kuwait is one of the world's richest countries per capita due to its vast oil reserves and is known for its modern architecture.
history: Kuwait gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1961.

name: Uzbekistan
funFact: Uzbekistan is home to Samarkand and Bukhara, ancient Silk Road cities renowned for their stunning Islamic architecture and tiles.
history: Uzbekistan gained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991.

name: Turkmenistan
funFact: Turkmenistan is home to the Darvaza gas crater, a burning natural gas field that has burned continuously for over 50 years.
history: Turkmenistan gained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991.

name: Kyrgyzstan
funFact: Kyrgyzstan is known as the 'Mountain Country' with over 90% of its territory covered by mountains, including parts of the Tian Shan range.
history: Kyrgyzstan gained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991.

name: Georgia
funFact: Georgia is believed to be where wine was first created, with evidence of winemaking dating back over 8,000 years.
history: Georgia gained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991.

name: Armenia
funFact: Armenia was the first country to officially adopt Christianity as its state religion in 301 AD.
history: Armenia gained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991, though it also briefly declared independence in 1918.

name: Bhutan
funFact: Bhutan measures progress by Gross National Happiness instead of GDP and prohibits tobacco sales to protect public health.
history: Bhutan was never colonized and has been ruled by its own monarchy since it was unified in 1907.

name: Croatia
funFact: Croatia's Plitvice Lakes National Park has 16 terraced lakes connected by waterfalls, all colored different shades of blue and green by mineral deposits.
history: Croatia declared independence from Yugoslavia in 1991, a move that helped trigger the Yugoslav Wars.

name: Serbia
funFact: Belgrade, Serbia's capital, sits at the confluence of the Danube and Sava rivers and has been destroyed and rebuilt over 40 times.
history: Serbia's independence from the Ottoman Empire was recognized in 1878; it became a separate state again in 2006 after Montenegro's split.

name: Slovenia
funFact: Slovenia is one of Europe's greenest countries, with over 60% of its territory covered by forests.
history: Slovenia declared independence from Yugoslavia in 1991 after only a brief Ten-Day War.

name: Albania
funFact: Albania is home to Lake Ohrid, one of Europe's deepest and oldest lakes, shared with North Macedonia.
history: Albania declared independence from the Ottoman Empire in 1912.

name: North Macedonia
funFact: North Macedonia is home to Lake Ohrid, which is one of the world's oldest freshwater lakes with unique endemic fish species.
history: North Macedonia declared independence from Yugoslavia in 1991, the only former Yugoslav republic to do so without violence at the time.

name: Romania
funFact: Romania is home to the Carpathian Mountains and is believed to be the inspiration for Bram Stoker's Dracula.
history: Romania formed in 1859 by uniting Wallachia and Moldavia, and gained full independence from the Ottoman Empire in 1878.

name: Bulgaria
funFact: Bulgaria is the world's leading producer of rose oil, producing about 70% of the global supply.
history: Bulgaria gained autonomy from the Ottoman Empire in 1878 and declared full independence in 1908.

name: Slovakia
funFact: Slovakia is home to the High Tatras mountains and has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites per capita than most European countries.
history: Slovakia became independent in 1993 in the peaceful 'Velvet Divorce' split from Czechoslovakia.

name: Lithuania
funFact: Lithuania is home to the Hill of Crosses, a unique pilgrimage site where thousands of crosses have been placed over centuries.
history: Lithuania was the first Soviet republic to declare independence from the USSR, in 1990, having also been briefly independent between 1918 and 1940.

name: Latvia
funFact: Latvia's capital Riga is known for its outstanding Art Nouveau architecture and has one of the widest waterfalls in Europe.
history: Latvia regained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991, having also been independent from 1918 to 1940.

name: Estonia
funFact: Estonia is a leader in digital innovation and e-governance, being one of the first countries to offer digital residency.
history: Estonia regained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991, having also been independent from 1918 to 1940.

name: Luxembourg
funFact: Luxembourg is one of the richest countries in the world per capita and is known for its medieval old town and picturesque castles.
history: Luxembourg gained independence from the Netherlands in 1839 under the Treaty of London.

name: Honduras
funFact: The Bay Islands off Honduras's Caribbean coast sit on the second-largest barrier reef system in the world, after Australia's Great Barrier Reef.
history: Honduras gained independence from Spain in 1821 as part of Central America's joint declaration.

name: Nicaragua
funFact: Nicaragua is home to Lake Nicaragua, the largest lake in Central America, which contains freshwater sharks.
history: Nicaragua gained independence from Spain in 1821.

name: El Salvador
funFact: El Salvador is the smallest country in Central America and the most densely populated.
history: El Salvador gained independence from Spain in 1821.

name: Belize
funFact: Belize is the only Central American country with English as the official language and is home to the Belize Barrier Reef, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
history: Belize gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1981, the last Central American country to do so.

name: Dominican Republic
funFact: The Dominican Republic occupies two-thirds of the island of Hispaniola and is known for its beautiful beaches and mountain ranges.
history: The Dominican Republic gained independence from Haiti in 1844, having earlier been a Spanish and then Haitian-ruled colony.

name: Haiti
funFact: Haiti was the first independent nation in Latin America and the Caribbean, and the first Black-led republic in the world.
history: Haiti gained independence from France in 1804 after a successful slave revolt, becoming the world's first Black-led republic.

name: Bahamas
funFact: The Bahamas is an archipelago of more than 700 islands and cays and is famous for its crystal-clear waters.
history: The Bahamas gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1973.

name: Trinidad and Tobago
funFact: Trinidad and Tobago is the southernmost Caribbean nation and is known for its diverse cultural heritage and Carnival celebration.
history: Trinidad and Tobago gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1962.

name: Paraguay
funFact: Paraguay is one of only a few countries in the world where an indigenous language, Guarani, is spoken by a majority of the population alongside Spanish.
history: Paraguay gained independence from Spain in 1811, one of the earliest South American colonies to do so.

name: Guyana
funFact: Guyana is the only English-speaking country in South America and is known for its pristine rainforests and biodiversity.
history: Guyana gained independence from the United Kingdom in 1966.

name: Suriname
funFact: Suriname is the smallest country in South America and the only Dutch-speaking country on the continent.
history: Suriname gained independence from the Netherlands in 1975.
